sourceRef: tip:Documenting exceptions so future you trusts them

slides.headline: Save Time by Documenting Exceptions
slides.body: Learn how to document exceptions effectively to ensure future compliance.

slides.headline: Understand the Current Guidance
slides.body: Review your SAA letter for specific rules on documenting exceptions.

slides.headline: Document Clearly
slides.body: Use a concise format that future you will understand. For example, 'Student failed to submit required documentation on time.'

slides.headline: Use Consistent Language
slides.body: Consistent terminology helps avoid confusion. For instance, 'Documentation not submitted' vs. 'Documentation late'.

slides.headline: Date and Sign
slides.body: Date and sign all documentation to establish responsibility and accountability.

slides.headline: Review and Update Regularly
slides.body: Regularly review your documentation to ensure it remains accurate and up-to-date.
Continue learning by exploring more compliance tips. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Learn how to document exceptions effectively to ensure future compliance. Get tips on understanding current guidance, using clear language, and maintaining consistent documentation. Regular reviews and updates are key. Explore more compliance tips. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 3, 'Use Consistent Language,' as the text is a bit dense and could benefit from a more visual element to break up the information.
